title: Organic acid derivatization protocol for mass spectrometry analysis
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"4g101","text":"This protocol outlines the derivatization and the detection of organic acids using mass spectrometry analysis. This technique is highly sensitive and allow the detection of low-concentration samples.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"25ue8","text":"Prepare the following solutions in 66% MS-grade acetronitrile: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1mmoo","text":"200 mM 3-NPH HCl in ACN 66%","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b5l22","text":"50 mM EDC in ACN 66%","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8ejhg","text":"7% pyridine in ACN 66%","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8vfbv","text":"5% formic acid (in MS-grade water) ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Dilute the samples if necessary (This technique is highly sensitive and allows the analysis of samples with low concentration). Caution: MOPS (3-( N -morpholino)propanesulfonic acid) saturates 3-NPH, preventing proper derivatization of organic acids. A dilution is necessary to achieve a maximum MOPS concentration of 1 mM
Prepare the reaction mixture: Pipette 50 µL of the sample. Add 25 µL of 200 mM 3-NPH HCl. Add 25 µL of 50 mM EDC. Add 25 µL of 7% pyridine solution.
Prepare the following solutions in 66% MS-grade acetronitrile (ACN): 200 mM 3-NPH HCl in ACN 66% 50 mM EDC in ACN 66% 7% pyridine in ACN 66% 5% formic acid (in MS-grade water)
Incubate the samples at 30°C for 5 hours at 500 rpm.
Add 125 µL of 5% formic acid to stop the reaction
creator: Manon Gilson
